title: State ordering guarantees explicitly
description: Ordering holds only within a partition/key; cross-partition order is not guaranteed and must not be assumed.
body: Use when consumer logic depends on event order (created before updated). Do not assume global ordering across a topic with multiple partitions or across topics. Actions: choose a partition key (e.g. aggregate id) so all events for one entity land on one partition and stay ordered; for cross-entity flows, design handlers to tolerate out-of-order arrival or use a sequence/version field. Validation signals include a test that two events for the same key are applied in order and that an out-of-order arrival for different keys is handled. Risk boundary: assuming order where the broker gives none causes silent state corruption.
